config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu itu yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh negara atau mayoritas. Di Indonesia, saya melihat mudah sekali alasan "demi kepentingan umum" dipakai untuk membenarkan keputusan yang merugikan warga kecil. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan individu sebagai korban demi alasan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because that is exactly how governments and majorities end up trampling basic rights. From an American perspective, our history makes that danger very real: free speech, due process, religious freedom, and equal protection exist precisely because the individual needs a shield against the collective. That said, individual rights are not a license to ignore public safety or common rules, so there has to be a balance. But when the choice is framed as a blanket rule, I come down firmly on the side of protecting the individual first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh dijadikan alasan otomatis untuk mengalahkan hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya justru sering melihat dalih “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, sementara yang punya kuasa tetap aman; ini membuat saya curiga pada klaim kolektif yang terlalu absolut. Hak individu itu bukan lawan masyarakat, melainkan pagar supaya mayoritas dan negara tidak semena-mena. Jadi, yang benar menurut saya adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, tetapi bila ada benturan serius, hak dasar individu harus tetap punya bobot yang sangat kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically more important than the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because it gives too much power to governments and majorities, and our history shows how easily "the common good" can become a cover for abuse. I do think there are real cases where public safety or shared rules limit individual freedom, but those limits have to be narrow, justified, and accountable. If society always wins by default, then individual rights stop meaning much at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak seharusnya diprioritaskan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru curiga ketika frasa “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu mudah, karena dalam praktiknya sering warga kecil yang paling dulu dikorbankan sementara pihak berkuasa lolos dari tanggung jawab. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; hak itu pagar supaya negara dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Jadi, saya setuju bahwa ada situasi tertentu untuk pembatasan, tetapi kalau dijadikan prinsip umum bahwa masyarakat selalu lebih dulu, itu berbahaya dan tidak adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that slogan is dangerous because it is exactly the kind of logic that has been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse by governments and majorities. I do think society can sometimes limit individual freedom for public safety, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable, not a blank check. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then they are no longer real rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
